title: saussurea parviflora subsp. purpurata
intro: Saussurea parviflora subsp. purpurata, also known as Purple Inula or Yunnan Inula, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family, native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and Yunnan Province in China. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Saussurea parviflora subsp. purpurata include reducing inflammation, relieving fever, and promoting sweating, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and its roots and flowers are also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
